perturbation.original_text: The construction, validity and performance of this Agreement shall be governed in all respects by the laws of Japan.
perturbation.changed_text: The construction, validity and performance of this Agreement shall be governed in all respects by the laws of Delaware.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the governing law from Japan to Delaware introduces a legal contradiction because the agreement involves a Japanese company (R-Tech Ueno, Ltd.) and targets the acquisition of shares under Japanese law. This could violate mandatory provisions of Japanese corporate and securities law related to tender offers and shareholder protection.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Financial Instruments and Exchange Act (FIEL) of Japan
perturbation.law_explanation: Articles 27-2 through 27-22 of the FIEL govern tender offers in Japan. By specifying Delaware law, the contract attempts to circumvent mandatory Japanese regulations concerning tender offers for Japanese companies, potentially violating shareholder protection provisions and procedural requirements under the FIEL.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: ページが移転されました｜Page Moved : 日本銀行 Bank of Japan このページの本文へ移動 ページが移転されました｜Page Moved お探しのページは、URLが変更されました。 お手数ですが、 トップページ または サイトマップ から再度アクセスして下さい。 The URL(address) of your requested page has been changed. Please try accessing the information again from the top page or the site map.
perturbation.justification: The original contract specifies Japanese law, while the changed contract specifies Delaware law. The Perturbation Explanation states this introduces a legal contradiction because the agreement involves a Japanese company and the acquisition of shares under Japanese law, which could violate mandatory provisions of Japanese corporate and securities law. The Law Explanation specifies Articles 27-2 through 27-22 of the FIEL govern tender offers in Japan. By specifying Delaware law, the contract attempts to circumvent mandatory Japanese regulations concerning tender offers for Japanese companies, potentially violating shareholder protection provisions and procedural requirements under the FIEL. Scraped Law Snippet 2 confirms the existence of the Bank of Japan and implies a legal framework within Japan. Therefore, changing the governing law to Delaware likely contradicts Japanese law, as it attempts to avoid Japanese regulations related to tender offers and shareholder protection.

perturbation.original_text: Any dispute, action or proceeding arising out of or in connection with this Agreement, including any question regarding its existence, validity, binding effect, breach, amendment or termination shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the Tokyo District Court.
perturbation.changed_text: Any dispute, action or proceeding arising out of or in connection with this Agreement, including any question regarding its existence, validity, binding effect, breach, amendment or termination shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of a court selected by Sucampo Pharmaceuticals, Inc.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying the jurisdiction clause to allow Sucampo Pharmaceuticals, Inc. to unilaterally select the court contradicts principles of fair dispute resolution under Japanese law. This is especially problematic because it could be construed as an attempt to avoid mandatory jurisdiction for matters involving Japanese companies or shareholders.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Article 3-3 of the Code of Civil Procedure (Japan)
perturbation.law_citation: Article 3-3 of the Code of Civil Procedure (Japan)
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 3-3, while allowing for jurisdictional agreements, mandates fairness. Granting only one party the right to select the jurisdiction is likely unenforceable under Japanese procedural law because it creates an unfair disadvantage for the other party, R-Tech Ueno, Ltd., especially when the contract is primarily centered around a Japanese company and subject to Japanese law.

perturbation.justification: The modification introduces a significant legal contradiction. The original contract specified the Tokyo District Court as the exclusive jurisdiction, ensuring a neutral venue. The changed text grants Sucampo Pharmaceuticals, Inc. the unilateral right to select the court. The Law Explanation states that this creates an unfair disadvantage for R-Tech Ueno, Ltd. The Perturbation Explanation also indicates that this alteration contradicts principles of fair dispute resolution, potentially avoiding mandatory jurisdiction. Although no specific law snippet directly prohibits this, the provided explanations of Japanese law suggest such a clause is likely unenforceable due to its inherent unfairness. Therefore, the modified text undermines the principle of fair dispute resolution, rendering the contract potentially non-compliant.
